findings.message: Missing return statement on the happy path: when `item` is found (truthy), the function falls off the end and implicitly returns None. FastAPI will then fail to serialize None against `response_model=ItemRead`, producing a 500 (ResponseValidationError) for every valid lookup. Only the 404 branch ever returns a value (via raise).
findings.suggestion: Add `return item` after the `if not item:` block so a successful lookup actually returns the ORM object.

findings.message: Using `if not item:` is the wrong null-check idiom for an ORM result. `session.get()` returns either the instance or None; an explicit `is None` check is correct and self-documents intent. While SQLAlchemy ORM instances aren't generally falsy, code review and future model __bool__/__len__ overrides can make `not item` unsafe — the in-code comment already flags this as a bug.
findings.suggestion: Replace `if not item:` with `if item is None:` to make the None-check explicit and robust against any future `__bool__`/`__len__` on the model.
